name: Accordion
description: Accordion lets operators reveal detail on demand — collapsing a long record or settings page into named sections that open one at a time or several at once, so the screen stays manageable. It's always composed with two sub-parts as children: AccordionSummary (the always-visible clickable header) and AccordionDetails (the revealed body).
keyRule: Use Accordion to tame dense detail (vehicle specs, settings groups). For switching between peer views of one record, prefer Tabs.
correction: Replaces the old MDC-modeled 'ExpansionPanel'. MDC Web never shipped an accordion primitive — the old component mirrored a custom Cartrack markup (.accordion/.hd/.bd2) instead of a real library contract. The real fleet-web component is MUI's Accordion.

variants.name: Single-open group
variants.whenToUse: Only one section open at a time (guided reading). Not built into Accordion itself — achieved by the parent lifting state and only ever setting one instance's `expanded` prop to true at a time (the classic MUI 'controlled group' pattern).

variants.name: Multi-open group
variants.whenToUse: Several sections open at once (reference scanning). Achieved by giving each Accordion instance its own independent `expanded`/`onChange` (or leaving it uncontrolled with `defaultExpanded`).

variants.name: With summary text
variants.whenToUse: Show a key value on the collapsed header — put it inside AccordionSummary alongside (or instead of) the title, e.g. a secondary <Typography> next to the heading.

anatomy.name: Accordion (root)
anatomy.description: the outer container. Renders on a Paper root by default (elevation, radius) and manages expanded/collapsed state via `expanded`/`onChange` or `defaultExpanded`.

anatomy.name: AccordionSummary (sub-component)
anatomy.description: the always-visible header — a separate karoo-ui/MUI component (@mui/material/AccordionSummary), not a prop of Accordion. Takes its own `expandIcon` prop (typically an ExpandMoreIcon) and toggles the parent Accordion's expanded state when clicked.

anatomy.name: AccordionDetails (sub-component)
anatomy.description: the panel body revealed when expanded — also a separate component (@mui/material/AccordionDetails), rendered as a plain content container with default padding.

anatomy.name: Expand icon
anatomy.description: passed via AccordionSummary's `expandIcon` prop; MUI rotates it 180° on expand via CSS, it isn't Accordion's own concern.

behaviors.name: Expand/collapse
behaviors.description: Clicking AccordionSummary toggles the Accordion's expanded state (controlled via `expanded`+`onChange`, or uncontrolled via `defaultExpanded`); the expand icon rotates via CSS to reflect state.

behaviors.name: Single vs multi (composition, not a prop)
behaviors.description: Accordion has no built-in accordion-group exclusivity. Whether opening one section closes its siblings is entirely up to the parent: render each Accordion controlled, and in the shared onChange handler decide whether to close the others.

behaviors.name: Deep linking
behaviors.description: A section can open automatically when linked to by setting its `expanded` prop from a route/query param — same controlled pattern as single/multi grouping.

states.treatment: AccordionSummary only, min-height ~48px; expand icon unrotated.
AccordionDetails visible; expand icon rotated 180°; AccordionSummary's min-height typically increases (~64px) unless `disableGutters` is set.
`disabled` prop on Accordion — AccordionSummary becomes non-interactive and dims via the theme's disabled text/icon opacity.
AccordionSummary is ButtonBase-backed — visible focus ring, hover state layer.

specs.value: Inherited from Paper (Accordion's root): semantic.radius.default (4px); default elevation per theme

Whatever is placed inside AccordionSummary — not a fixed variant. The old doc's 'Roboto 14–16px/500' claim was the MDC spec's guess, not a real constraint; this theme's default text variant is body2 (14px/400) unless you set your own Typography variant inside.

props.name: children
props.description: Typically exactly one AccordionSummary followed by one AccordionDetails. Both are separate real components (@mui/material/AccordionSummary, @mui/material/AccordionDetails) composed as children — not props of Accordion.

props.name: expanded
props.description: Controlled open state. Setting this enables control over the accordion and is required to implement single-open-vs-multi-open group behavior in a parent.

props.name: defaultExpanded
props.description: Uncontrolled initial expanded state. Default false.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Fired when the expand/collapse state changes (i.e. AccordionSummary was clicked).

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the whole accordion (its AccordionSummary becomes non-interactive). Default false.

props.name: disableGutters
props.description: Removes the margin between two expanded accordion items and prevents the increased min-height when expanded. Default false.

props.name: square
props.description: Paper prop, inherited: if true, removes the rounded corners.

props.name: TransitionProps
props.description: Props forwarded to the internal Collapse transition (e.g. timeout). Newer MUI versions prefer `slotProps.transition` for the same purpose.

doThis: Name sections clearly so collapsed AccordionSummary headers are scannable.
Put a summary value inside AccordionSummary where it helps.
Decide and keep consistent whether a group is single-open or multi-open by how you wire the controlled `expanded` props.
dontDoThis: Don't hide critical, always-needed info behind a collapse.
Don't use Accordion where Tabs fit better (peer views, not layered detail).
accessibility: AccordionSummary renders as a heading (h3 by default via the `heading` slot) containing a button-like control exposing aria-expanded.
AccordionDetails is associated via aria-controls / a region role so assistive tech can find the revealed content.
Fully keyboard operable with visible focus on AccordionSummary.
The expand icon is decorative — state is conveyed programmatically via aria-expanded, not by the icon's rotation alone.

related.name: Drawer
related.why: Details that deserve their own surface (replaces the old doc's 'SideSheet' — SideSheet duplicate-maps to Drawer)

related.name: Tabs
related.why: Peer views of the same subject
